docs.title: Index
docs.text: Tidy Tools is a declarative programming library promoting functional PySpark DataFrame workflows. The package is an extension of the PySpark API and can be easily integrated into existing code.

docs.title: Key features
docs.text:
• Fast: Written from scratch in Rust, designed close to the machine and without external dependencies.
  • I/O: First class support for all common data storage layers: local, cloud storage & databases.
  • Intuitive API: Write your queries the way they were intended. Polars, internally, will determine the most efficient way to execute using its query optimizer.
  • Out of Core: The streaming API allows you to process your results without requiring all your data to be in memory at the same time.
  • Parallel: Utilises the power of your machine by dividing the workload among the available CPU cores without any additional configuration.
  • Vectorized Query Engine: Using Apache Arrow, a columnar data format, to process your queries in a vectorized manner and SIMD to optimize CPU usage.
  • GPU Support: Optionally run queries on NVIDIA GPUs for maximum performance for in-memory workloads.

docs.title: Philosophy
docs.text:
The goal of Tidy Tools is to provide an extension of the PySpark DataFrame API that:

  • Utilizes all available cores on your machine.
  • Optimizes queries to reduce unneeded work/memory allocations.
  • Handles datasets much larger than your available RAM.
  • A consistent and predictable API.
  • Adheres to a strict schema (data-types should be known before running the query).

On top of the existing API, Tidy Tools provides recipes for converting PySpark expressions into tidy expressions.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: All contributions are welcome, from reporting bugs to implementing new features. Read our contributing guide to learn more.

docs.title: License
docs.text: This project is licensed under the terms of the MIT license.

docs.title: Three Packages in One
docs.text:
Tidy Tools is a collection of three modules: - core: packages recipes convenient for manipulating data - frame: extends the PySpark DataFrame to include built-in logging - models: introduces schema-level definitions of dataframes and workflows

By default, all modules can be used on their own without explicitly referring to the others. However, when used together, PySpark workflows are simple yet powerful.
